message:
chore(beads): mark config schema pane priorities task (bd-3lai) as in_progress

Updates the issue tracker to reflect that the config schema work for
per-pane priorities and capture budgets has been picked up.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-3iar","title":"Unit tests: saved searches + scheduler","description":"## Coverage
- Storage CRUD and deterministic ordering
- Scheduler interval logic + rate limiting
- Alert payload redaction and counts

## Logging
- Log scheduled run timestamps and decision branches
- Log alert payloads with redaction markers

## Success Criteria
- Tests cover edge cases (empty results, disabled search, invalid scope)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:02:03.478637389Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T03:02:48.510234972Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3iar","depends_on_id":"bd-1t2b","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:02:03.491734409Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3iar","depends_on_id":"bd-37f9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:02:44.584938948Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3iar","depends_on_id":"bd-4enj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:02:48.510205477Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3iax","title":"IPC config + socket lifecycle design","description":"## What
Define IPC config options and socket lifecycle management.

## Why
We need safe defaults and predictable file paths for local clients.

## How
- Config: enabled flag, socket path, permissions
- Lifecycle: create/remove socket, handle stale sockets

## Success Criteria
- Socket path is deterministic and safe
- Startup/teardown handles crashes cleanly","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:19:24.280472849Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T03:19:24.287205588Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3iax","depends_on_id":"bd-15ds","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:19:24.287170263Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3kzu","title":"TUI/Web surfaces for saved searches","description":"## What
Expose saved searches in TUI and web dashboards.

## Why
Operators should be able to view/trigger saved searches without memorizing CLI flags.

## How
- TUI: list saved searches, run on demand, toggle enabled
- Web: read-only list + last run status (no mutations yet)

## Risks
- Feature gating: keep behind tui/web feature flags

## Success Criteria
- Saved searches visible in TUI and web
- No direct DB reads from UI; use shared query client","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:01:54.854781244Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T05:16:37.424847534Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3kzu","depends_on_id":"bd-1t2b","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:01:54.864273971Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3kzu","depends_on_id":"bd-37f9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:02:30.257461340Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3kzu","depends_on_id":"bd-4enj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:02:33.800434310Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3kzu","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T05:16:37.424812299Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3kzu","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T05:16:35.220851055Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-3lai","title":"Config schema: pane priorities + budgets","description":"## What
Define config fields for per-pane priorities and capture budgets.

## Why
Operators need explicit, documented knobs to tune capture behavior.

## How
- Add config entries for default priority + per-pane overrides
- Add budget settings (max captures/sec, max bytes/sec)
- Validation + defaults for backward compatibility

## Success Criteria
- Config validates and loads with sensible defaults
- Documentation includes examples","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:04:37.625306400Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T03:04:37.643709418Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3lai","depends_on_id":"bd-396b","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:04:37.643654756Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3m3a","title":"E2E: CLI polish validation with verbose logs","description":"## Goal
Validate shell completions, aliases, and progressive disclosure end-to-end with detailed logging.

## Requirements
- Exercise completions and aliases in a scripted shell session with verbose logs enabled.
- Verify help output and verbosity tiers are stable (default/verbose/debug).
- Capture logs, command outputs, and completion snapshots as artifacts on failure.

## Acceptance Criteria
- E2E CLI polish scenario passes locally and in CI.
- Failure artifacts include full logs, completion outputs, and help snapshots.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T20:20:47.793107080Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:10:18.766547718Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T23:10:18.766490961Z","close_reason":"Created scripts/e2e_cli_polish.sh - comprehensive E2E tests for CLI polish features. Tests help output consistency (10 commands), verbosity tiers, command aliases, output consistency, help text quality, error messages, and progressive disclosure. 38 tests pass, 15 skipped for unimplemented wa-rnf features.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3m3a","depends_on_id":"bd-35zb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:09:58Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3m3a","depends_on_id":"bd-u194","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:09:58Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3m3a","depends_on_id":"wa-rnf","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:09:58Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3p04","title":"Add wa robot approve command","description":"# wa robot approve

## Current State (CLI Stub Done)
✅ CLI subcommand structure added
✅ JSON schema created (wa-robot-approve.json)
❌ Implementation returns not-implemented error

## What Remains
Integrate with ApprovalStore to validate and consume tokens.

## Implementation
1. Accept approval code parameter
2. Query ApprovalStore for token
3. Validate workspace scope, expiry, fingerprint
4. Mark as consumed on success
5. Record in audit trail

## Testing Requirements
- Unit: Valid code consumes token and returns success
- Unit: Expired codes return E_APPROVAL_EXPIRED
- Unit: Already-consumed codes return E_APPROVAL_CONSUMED
- Unit: Wrong pane/workspace returns appropriate error
- E2E: Full approval flow (request → grant → consume)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Validates approval codes via ApprovalStore
- [ ] Consumes token on success
- [ ] All error cases return stable error codes
- [ ] Audit trail records approval consumption
- [ ] JSON validates against schema
- [ ] Unit + E2E tests pass with detailed logging

Related: bd-39wn (detailed implementation spec)
Blocked by: wa-4vx.8.9 (approval token infrastructure)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-22T18:21:36.255366399Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T19:43:18.249135961Z","closed_at":"2026-01-22T19:43:18.249089584Z","close_reason":"Duplicate of bd-39wn which is more detailed (has error codes, pane context, relationship to human command)","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3p04","depends_on_id":"bd-39wn","type":"relates-to","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:09:58Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3p04","depends_on_id":"bd-h1ai","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:09:58Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3p04","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.8.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:09:58Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":4,"issue_id":"bd-3p04","author":"ubuntu","text":"Reopened: CLI stub only - full implementation pending ApprovalStore integration","created_at":"2026-01-22T18:53:16Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3p06","title":"Auth tokens + capability scopes for IPC","description":"## What
Implement token-based authentication and capability scopes for local IPC.

## Why
Local IPC should not be a privilege escalation path.

## How
- Generate short-lived tokens with scope (read-only vs mutating)
- Validate tokens on every request
- Reuse PolicyEngine for mutating actions

## Success Criteria
- Invalid/expired tokens rejected with stable errors
- Scope enforced for each method","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:19:33.145217948Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T03:20:13.609931520Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3p06","depends_on_id":"bd-15ds","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:19:33.152820373Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3p06","depends_on_id":"bd-3iax","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:20:13.609902236Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
